Default Template.content:
Dear [MR/MS HR LAST NAME], 

I am writing to express my strong interest in the [Role Name] position at [Company Name]. As a Computer Science student at Stony Brook University graduating in 2026, I have been following [Company Name]’s work in [mention a specific product, feature, or mission of theirs]. I am particularly drawn to your team's focus on [mention a specific value they have, e.g., scalability, user-centric design, or innovation], as it mirrors my own passion for building software that solves tangible problems.
In my recent internships, I learned that effective engineering is about more than just writing clean code; it is about driving business impact. At Opportunity Knocks, I didn't just implement features; I focused on how those features—like a new subscription system—could directly translate to revenue and user retention. This experience taught me to prioritize engineering decisions that balance technical debt with immediate business needs, a mindset I am eager to bring to the engineering team at [Company Name].
Beyond my professional work, I am passionate about building full-stack applications that leverage emerging technologies. Recently, I built [TutorAI / ResuMaker], where I focused heavily on [mention a challenge: e.g., minimizing latency for AI voice responses OR creating an intuitive UX for document management]. This project sharpened my ability to learn new tools like [Supabase / Next.js / OpenAI API] quickly and implement them effectively to improve the end-user experience.
I am confident that my technical foundation in full-stack development, combined with my adaptability and focus on product quality, would make me a valuable addition to your team. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to the possibility of discussing how I can contribute to [Company Name]’s continued success.


Sincerely,
Benjamin Tang
placeholders: mention a challenge: e.g., minimizing latency for AI voice responses OR creating an intuitive UX for document management
mention a specific product, feature, or mission of theirs
mention a specific value they have, e.g., scalability, user-centric design, or innovation
